comment.body:
SPX Game Plan – Wed, Apr 02  
(Liberation Day! Except Trump called his announcement for 4 PM, so we're really in the holding cell awaiting our discharge papers and the conditions of our parole)

—

CONTEXT  
• Yesterday = wide swings + EOD dealer rally  
• GEX still thin & fragmented both ways → dealers short puts & calls  
• Overnight: 5600–5650 | E-mini straddle implies 5550–5650 range  
• Catalysts: ADP private payrolls + 4PM Trump presser  
• Current structure favors chop, large swings, tail reactivity  
• 5600–5620 = high chop risk / neutral zone  
• 5540–5505 = unstable footing  
• 5640–5670 = resistance shelf

—

KEY LEVELS & STRUCTURE  
• Put OI: Far OTM 5505↓, 5505–5550 OTM, 5575–95 NTM, 5615/40 ITM  
• Call OI: 5550/60 call debit spread = support/pivot, 5600 NTM, 5660+ OTM  
• Strong GEX Zones: 5500/05, 5550/60, 5640, 5695  
• 20D Strikes: 5540P / 5655C (monitor those deltas intraday — they’ll shift with changes in SPX spot and IV)  
• Tue Range: 5555–5645 | WTD: 5490–5650  
• 5600 area = trap-prone; needs volume or vol crush for escape

—

SPX ROAD MAP – 0DTE  
Chop Zone: 5560–5620  
• 5600 = Pivot BMO  
• IF sticky → path to 5615 or 5580  
• < 5580/85 → 5560 magnet; may stall ~5570 before continuation  
• > 5600 → grind to 5615/20

Above:  
• > 5620 → test 5630/35 → 5640  
• > 5640 → 5650 round number  
• > 5650 → stall near 5660, stretch target = 5676/5700

Below:  
• = 5560 → reversal or continuation  
• < 5560 → 5550  
• < 5550 → 5540 (gamma flip = unstable)  
• < 5540 → 5520/25 → 5505/00 → reversal zone toward 5550

—

VOLATILITY & POSITIONING INSIGHTS  
VIX Structure (6:45 AM):  
VIX1D (20) < VIX9D (24.5) > $VIX (22.9) > VIX3M (21.8) > VX (21.5)  
VVIX = 97.5  
• Short-term backwardation → elevated front vol, mixed message beyond  
• 9D pumped for tariffs/NFP/political risk  
• Spot vol in the middle → market unsure  
• VVIX elevated = wings sticky, not decompressing cleanly  
• VOLI + TDEX softening, but far OTM tails still bid  
• Expect vol to stay bid through early session → decompress possible post-4PM

—

TRADE STRUCTURING & EXECUTION  
• Scalp bias around 5600 pivot  
• Charm fuel potential >5550/60  
• Fade edges unless vol compresses  
• Avoid chasing >5650 unless clean break + vol drops  
• Reversal setups near 5540 or 5670 edges

—

POST-TRADE REVIEW & ADJUSTMENTS  
• Monitor reactions at 5600 → 5615 or 5580  
• Charm + EOD rally >5615 possible if vol drops  
• Watch for ADP fake + fade → macro flip risk

—

ADDITIONAL CONSIDERATIONS  
• GEX profile = thin, wingy, reactive  
• 5600–5620 = chop trap zone  
• 5640–5670 = supply shelf  
• 5540–5505 = prone to slippery downside extensions  
• Vol compressible after 4PM if presser underwhelms

—

Wider Levels / MTF Notes  
• Weekly range: 5490–5650  
• Above: 5660 → 5676 → 5700  
• Below: 5525 → 5505 → 5490  
• Gamma flip zone = 5540  
• 20D inflection still key: 5540P / 5655C  
• Monitor those 20D deltas intraday — they’ll shift with changes in SPX spot and IV.

comment.body: So upside intraday mean reversion is starting to fade, but we still need lower RV to realize a drop. If we remain below 5722 this is just common mean reversion trading that doesn’t mean we are on the way higher. However, if we are above 5722 and volatility (vix and RV?) is down, then we might see higher prices continuing a more convincing “bull trap” rally? Goal is for 20RV on the Trading View indicator to reach about 16% for lower volatility requirements to be realized. If we get that above 5722 and it is lower than that, just icing on the cake to further confirm eventual pending downside?

comment.body: Its sorta illogical. Musk is cutting government spending, which as long as the savings are used to reduce the deficit is a cut of 1% of GDP so far. Trump is raising Tariffs, folk mistakenly see this as inflationary. It is not. Imagine you live in an economy with 3 consumers with £120 and one supplier of food (banana's). The banana supplier has 6 bananas. what is the price of banana's...£20each.  Now the ffod supplier trys to incraese prices to £30....but there is still only £120 in the economy....he either lets the banana's rot or he has to reduce his price...... Now if you introduce a bank that has £120 to loan what is the price of bananas then.....£40. Its the increase in money supply that increases prices not the price of the product. If the price increases with same amount of money in supply it either rots or prices fall back to normal

comment.body:
Has anyone read about China’s dominance in auto manufacturing? They are the largest auto exporter in the world now and absolutely dominating their sphere of influence. What is Mercedes or BMW gonna do? Sell a $60K car to a family in Africa or Vietnam or India?

They better start producing more economical makes/models if they want to compete.

And is China gonna let them access their middle/upper class? Highly doubt it.
